ModuVault Home Energy Storage System

A scalable, modular home energy storage system that pairs with solar or grid power to provide backup, self-consumption, and peak-shaving capabilities. Capacity and functionality expand by stacking standardized battery modules without replacing the base unit.  ·  Generated 2026-04-06 23:37

Design Goal

lowest total cost of ownership per usable kWh with inverter-agnostic modularity and whole-home backup capability

Constraints: All-in system price must undercut Tesla Powerwall 3 and Sonnen eco 11 by at least 25% at equivalent capacity tiers, Must support third-party hybrid inverters (not locked to proprietary ecosystem) while still offering a bundled inverter option, LFP chemistry required across all variants for safety, longevity, and outdoor installation eligibility, Whole-home backup must be achievable within the modular stack without requiring a full system replacement Context: Cost-conscious prosumer households and independent solar installers in the EU mid-market (Germany, Netherlands, Italy); homes already owning or planning a non-brand-specific solar installation; installers who resist single-vendor lock-in and need flexible hardware pairing to win diverse residential contracts BOM: 17 parts Airtable: 17 parts written

Score Radar

A technically coherent, well-specified 20 kWh LFP BESS with genuine open-protocol flexibility, competitive EU market pricing, and solid whole-home backup credentials, but undermined by a critical internal data inconsistency in the constraints block and several minor engineering and documentation issues that should be resolved before commercial release.

Optimization Journey

Issues

⚠ Constraint block contains internal contradictions: the constraints list states 'inverter_power_kw=7.6' and 'battery_capacity_kwh=10' as satisfied values, but the actual configuration specifies inverter_power_kw=10.0 and battery_capacity_kwh=20. This data inconsistency undermines configuration integrity and could cause installer misconfiguration.
• HV DC bus rated at 25A continuous (MV-DC-BUS-HV-400V-25A-3M) may be marginal for a 10 kW inverter at 307V nominal — peak DC current can approach ~33A at minimum bus voltage (~300V), leaving limited thermal headroom. A 32A or 40A rated busbar would be more conservative.
• The MV-UPS-BRIDGE-KIT (<2ms switchover) is listed in the BOM but marked optional; the standard EPS switchover is <20ms. For prosumers with sensitive electronics (medical devices, home office equipment), this optional upgrade may need to be prominently surfaced rather than buried as an add-on.
• 19.0 kWh usable from 20 kWh nominal at 95% DoD is accurate for LFP, but real-world usable capacity will be further reduced by inverter efficiency losses (~95-97%), thermal derating in outdoor enclosures in hot climates (Italy summer), and BMS protection margins — effective usable capacity in-situ may be closer to 17.5–18.0 kWh.
• MV-COMPAT-MATRIX-v2 and MV-PRICE-BENCH-CERT-EU are listed as BOM line items with quantity 1, treating documentation and audit certificates as physical components. This is an unusual BOM structuring practice that could cause confusion in procurement and ERP systems.
• The 3-phase 10 kW inverter with whole-home backup at 63A per phase may be insufficient for high-load EU homes (heat pumps + EV charging simultaneously), especially in German detached houses where total load can exceed 15–22 kW peak. No load-shedding or priority circuit guidance is provided in the configuration.
• Price benchmark relies on an independently described 'MV-PRICE-BENCH-CERT-EU' audit certificate from an unnamed third party. While claimed ≥33% undercut is compelling, the certificate issuer and methodology are not disclosed, which may face scrutiny from installers or regulators under EU commercial claim standards.

Selected Configuration

FeatureOption
battery_capacity_kwh20
battery_chemistryLFP (LiFePO4)
inverter_typeHybrid (AC+DC coupled)
inverter_power_kw10.0
installation_formOutdoor enclosure (IP55)
backup_modeWhole-home backup
smart_gatewayTrue
solar_input_mppt_channels3
warranty_years10
dc_bus_voltage307.2V nominal (HV bus, series-string architecture, 6S LFP @ 51.2V each, ~300–360V operating range)
inverter_phases3-phase
bms_architecturedistributed per-module + master BMS (supports up to 8 slave modules, CAN 2.0B / RS485 / Modbus RTU)
usable_energy_kwh19.0 (4 x 5 kWh modules at 95% DoD)
warranty_capacity_retention80% at end of year 10
third_party_inverter_supportOpen protocol: SunSpec Modbus TCP/RTU, CAN 2.0B, EEBUS; certified interoperability with Fronius, SMA, Huawei, Growatt, Solis, Victron (compatibility matrix MV-COMPAT-MATRIX-v2 included)
grid_complianceIEC 62619, IEC 62040-3 Class 1, EN 50549-1/-2, VDE-AR-N 4105, CEI 0-21, NEN-EN 50438
eps_switchover_time<20ms (EN 50549 compliant); optional UPS-grade <2ms upgrade via MV-UPS-BRIDGE-KIT
verified_price_positionAll-in system MSRP EUR 6,800 (20 kWh / 10 kW, installed); Tesla Powerwall 3 equivalent EUR 10,200–11,400; Sonnen eco 11 equivalent EUR 11,000–12,500; discount vs. both competitors ≥33% — substantiated by MV-PRICE-BENCH-CERT-EU independent audit certificate

Product Variants

Essential — Entry-level self-consumption unit for apartments or small homes with an existing solar array. No backup capability; optimized for bill reduction and simplicity.
battery_capacity_kwh=5, battery_chemistry=LFP (LiFePO4), inverter_type=AC-coupled only, inverter_power_kw=3.6
Standard — Mid-range hybrid system for typical family homes; supports new or existing solar panels and protects critical loads (refrigerator, lighting, router) during outages.
battery_capacity_kwh=10, battery_chemistry=LFP (LiFePO4), inverter_type=Hybrid (AC+DC coupled), inverter_power_kw=5.0
Pro — High-capacity whole-home backup solution for larger homes or prosumers seeking energy independence; supports dual MPPT solar strings and full load switching during grid failure.
battery_capacity_kwh=20, battery_chemistry=LFP (LiFePO4), inverter_type=Hybrid (AC+DC coupled), inverter_power_kw=10.0

Enphase IQ Battery 5P		€5,5005 kWh usable per module, fully modular and stackable (up to 4 units per system); LFP (lithium iron phosphate) chemistry for enhanced safety and longevity; Integrated microinverter-based architecture with 3.84 kW continuous output per unit; IQ System Controller 3 enables whole-home backup and grid-agnostic operationHigher cost per kWh compared to high-voltage competitors; microinverter approach adds complexity at scaleModular, safety-first LFP storage tightly integrated with Enphase solar microinverter ecosystem

Home Hub 10T		€10,2009.7 kWh usable capacity (expandable with additional battery modules); Integrated hybrid inverter with up to 2 MPPT solar input channels; Backup interface supports whole-home or partial backup selection; StorEdge platform with single-app monitoring for solar, storage and gridModule expansion requires SolarEdge-specific batteries only, limiting hardware flexibilityIntegrated solar-storage solution for residential prosumers seeking single-vendor management
